title: centaurea pomeliana subsp. rouxiana
intro: Centaurea pomeliana subsp. rouxiana, also known as Roux's knapweed, is a perennial plant in the daisy family, characterized by its yellow flowers and leafy stems. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, Centaurea pomeliana subsp. rouxiana is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be used topically as an ointment or salve to soothe skin irritations.
